scope: G group round 3 tactical review and predicted lineups
notes: This file contains predicted lineups only, not confirmed T-75 lineups.
This file does not provide final score forecasts or betting recommendations.

motivation_summary: Egypt have the clearest low-risk management path.
Belgium have the strongest need to push for a win.
Iran can stay compact early but may have to open up late.
New Zealand have limited slow-game value and remain outlet-and-set-piece dependent.

late_game_information_risk.evidence: Simultaneous kickoffs can change press height, block depth, and substitution windows after the hour mark.
late_game_information_risk.model_note: Do not treat the final phase as a static game state.

key_subs_and_triggers: New Zealand should add pace and second-ball support if they fall behind.
Belgium should add more box presence if still level after 60 minutes.
Belgium late-game risk rises if the parallel match remains level.

key_matchups.duel: Romelu Lukaku vs Michael Boxall/Finn Surman
key_matchups.importance: Belgium box occupation and cross conversion

key_matchups.duel: Chris Wood vs Brandon Mechele/Arthur Theate
key_matchups.importance: New Zealand outlet and second-ball access

key_matchups.duel: Kevin De Bruyne vs Joe Bell/Marko Stamenic

tactical_edges.edge: Wide isolation and half-space service should create sustained territorial control.
Lukaku start would raise box occupation quality more than raw volume.
Wood outlet play remains the best path into direct attacks and second balls.
Set pieces and aerial duels remain the main equalizer.

risk_points.risk: Finishing efficiency remains weaker than territorial control.
Transition defence becomes shakier if the game-state forces harder chasing.
Long defensive phases can expose the fullback zones and second-ball areas at the top of the box.

xg_adjustment_guidance.note: Raise Belgium shot-volume expectation but keep a finishing cap; keep a small high-value transition tail for New Zealand.

third_round_motivation_tactical_shift.model_note: Belgium have a stronger must-win push than in the first two rounds.

rotation_shape_risk.model_note: Neither side has enough points buffer for major rotation.

draw_management_tendency.model_note: A long draw-management script is unlikely for either team.

goal_difference_chase_risk.model_note: Can be triggered by the scoreline in the other match.

late_game_information_risk.model_note: Parallel scoreboard pressure can sharply change the final phase.

double_counting_warning: Do not fully stack Belgium territorial control and Doku wide advantage.
Do not fully stack Wood outlet value and New Zealand set-piece threat.

key_subs_and_triggers: Egypt should add protection layers if leading.
Iran should add direct runners and crossing support if they still need a goal after 60 minutes.
Iran's late-game risk rises if Belgium lead in the parallel match.

key_matchups.duel: Omar Marmoush/Emam Ashour vs Saeid Ezatolahi/Rouzbeh Cheshmi
Mehdi Taremi/Shahriyar Moghanlou vs Hamdy Fathy/Yasser Ibrahim
key_matchups.importance: Direct-play conversion into real box shots

key_matchups.duel: Ramin Rezaeian crossing lane vs Ahmed Fatouh recovery

tactical_edges.edge: Transition quality through Salah and Marmoush remains the most reliable strength.
If Iran's left side pushes higher, Egypt's counter space expands.
Compact box protection has already shown itself as a real repeatable trait.
Rezaeian crossing, Taremi link play, and Moghanlou aerial work remain the best route into the area.

risk_points.risk: A very deep early lead-protection script can cede second balls and crossing pressure.
Recovery speed and fullback space become clearer problems if they must chase.
The largest hidden variable is late simultaneous-score pressure changing the tempo.

xg_adjustment_guidance.note: Base game-state is more controlled, but Iran scoreboard pressure can lift late chance quality on both sides.

third_round_motivation_tactical_shift.model_note: Egypt have more draw-management room; Iran have more forced-upside risk.

rotation_shape_risk.model_note: Neither side has enough table security for major structural rotation.

draw_management_tendency.model_note: Egypt are the more plausible low-risk manager if the other match stays non-extreme.

goal_difference_chase_risk.model_note: This is conditional on the scoreline in the other match.

late_game_information_risk.model_note: Parallel-match information can reshape the final 25 to 30 minutes.

double_counting_warning: Do not fully stack Egypt transition lift, Salah form, and Iran late chasing into one oversized total-goal boost.
Do not fully restack Iran low-block value if it is already reflected in prior defensive sample inputs.

data_gaps: Official T-75 lineups are unavailable for both matches.
Belgium disciplinary confirmation after round two remains incomplete in local files.
Taremi and Mohebi are monitor-status players, not confirmed outs.
New Zealand pre-match fitness detail remains incomplete.
